posts.title: Director Selected for Prestigious WLSE Global Leadership Program
posts.excerpt: KnowledgePie is proud to announce that our Director has been selected among 20 distinguished women leaders in STEM for the Women Leadership in STEM Entrepreneurship (WLSE) program—an elite global initiative supported by the Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) under its Grand Challenges India (GCI).
longDescription: KnowledgePie is proud to announce that our Director has been selected among 20 distinguished women leaders in STEM for the Women Leadership in STEM Entrepreneurship (WLSE) program—an elite global initiative supported by the Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) under its Grand Challenges India (GCI).

The session brought together leaders, researchers, and students to discuss pathways for women in science and entrepreneurship, with a focus on building inclusive innovation ecosystems in the Northeast and beyond.

Participants shared experiences from academia, industry, and startups—highlighting mentorship, funding access, and the role of regional ecosystems in nurturing the next generation of women innovators.

images.caption: Director Selected for Prestigious WLSE Global Leadership Program
Director Selected for Prestigious WLSE Global Leadership Program

posts.title: Engaging Interaction with Forvis Mazars LLP under BIRAC BIO RIDE Initiative
posts.excerpt: KnowledgePie had an insightful interaction with Forvis Mazars LLP as part of the survey conducted under the BIO RIDE scheme by Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC).
longDescription: KnowledgePie had an insightful interaction with Forvis Mazars LLP as part of the survey conducted under the BIO RIDE scheme by Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC).
The discussion provided valuable perspectives and meaningful insights, contributing to a deeper understanding of the initiative and its impact.

posts.title: Odyssey – NEC IGNITE Conclave: Recognizing Innovation with Impact
posts.excerpt: KnowledgePie Pvt. Ltd. is proud to have been honoured with the “Research Translational Innovation to Impact Award” at the Odyssey – NEC IGNITE Conclave.
longDescription: KnowledgePie Pvt. Ltd. is proud to have been honoured with the “Research Translational Innovation to Impact Award” at the Odyssey – NEC IGNITE Conclave.

This recognition marks a significant milestone in our journey, reflecting our commitment to translating scientific research into real-world solutions, particularly in the field of healthcare innovation. It reinforces our mission to bridge the gap between research and impactful implementation.
We sincerely thank the organizers of the NEC IGNITE Conclave for curating a dynamic platform that celebrates innovation and entrepreneurship across North East India.
We are deeply grateful for the encouragement and insights shared by distinguished dignitaries, including Bamin Tarang and Abhinav Kumar, whose perspectives continue to inspire our work.

We also extend our appreciation to the entire NEC IGNITE ecosystem for their continued support and belief in our vision. KnowledgePie remains committed to scaling impactful innovations and contributing meaningfully to the evolving healthcare and startup ecosystem.

posts.title: SHE INSPIRES 2026 – Celebrating Women’s Day with Inspiration and Impact
posts.excerpt: KnowledgePie successfully hosted its first online webinar, SHE INSPIRES 2026, in celebration of International Women’s Day—marking an important milestone in our journey of fostering learning and empowerment.
longDescription: KnowledgePie successfully hosted its first online webinar, SHE INSPIRES 2026, in celebration of International Women’s Day—marking an important milestone in our journey of fostering learning and empowerment.

The session brought together an inspiring panel of accomplished women from diverse professional backgrounds, who shared their experiences, insights, and career journeys with the audience. Their stories of dedication, resilience, innovation, and leadership made the event highly engaging and impactful.

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to our esteemed speakers—Dr. Jashmini Deka, Dr. Kakoli Bhattacharya, Srija Perumalla, Lakhirupa Devi, and Tahmina Mazumder—for generously sharing their valuable perspectives and inspiring all participants.

At KnowledgePie, we remain committed to creating platforms that encourage learning, innovation, and inclusivity. We hope this session inspired many young minds to pursue their ambitions with confidence and explore new opportunities. We look forward to hosting many more meaningful and empowering discussions in the future

images.caption: SHE INSPIRES 2026 – Celebrating Women’s Day with Inspiration and Impact

posts.title: Advancing Healthcare Innovation at GMCH, Assam
posts.excerpt: KnowledgePie was honoured to participate in a landmark event at Gauhati Medical College & Hospital (GMCH), marking a significant step forward in Assam’s healthcare advancement.
longDescription: KnowledgePie was honoured to participate in a landmark event at Gauhati Medical College & Hospital (GMCH), marking a significant step forward in Assam’s healthcare advancement.

We extend our sincere gratitude to Hon’ble Chief Minister Shri Himanta Biswa Sarma and Shri Ashok Singhal, Hon’ble Minister of Health & Family Welfare, for their esteemed presence and encouraging interaction. The Hon’ble Chief Minister’s interest in our technology was truly motivating and reaffirmed our commitment to innovation. The event witnessed several key milestones, including the Bhumi Pujan of the new GMCH building, the inauguration of the Mother & Child Health Building, and the unveiling of indigenous healthcare innovations such as the Low-Field MRI prototype and Surgical Robot developed under the Assam Advanced Healthcare Innovation Institute (AAHII), supported by BioNEST IIT Guwahati.

We sincerely thank BioNEST IIT Guwahati for this opportunity and commend AAHII for its visionary efforts in advancing cutting-edge healthcare technologies in the region. The program also provided a valuable platform to engage with clinicians, students, the public, and fellow startups—fostering meaningful discussions around research, innovation, and future collaborations. KnowledgePie remains committed to contributing to the growth of Assam’s healthcare innovation ecosystem.

images.caption: Advancing Healthcare Innovation at GMCH, Assam

posts.title: Knowledgepie Wins 2nd Runner-Up at TiE Kolkata Women 2025 Finale
posts.excerpt: We are delighted to share a proud milestone for Knowledgepie Private Limited—our team secured 2nd runner-up at the TiE Kolkata Women 2025 finale.
longDescription: We are delighted to share a proud milestone for Knowledgepie Private Limited—our team secured 2nd runner-up at the TiE Kolkata Women 2025 finale.
TiE Kolkata Women celebrates women-led ventures driving impact in science and business. This recognition validates our mission to deliver safer MRI contrast through indigenous innovation.
The finale featured pitching sessions, mentorship circles, and networking with investors focused on healthcare and deep-tech startups.

posts.title: Exploring New Horizons: Our Inspiring Visit to Biophore and Vivere Imaging
posts.excerpt: We recently had the privilege of visiting Biophore India Pharmaceuticals and Vivere Imaging, exploring synergies that can advance safer MRI contrast solutions.
longDescription: We recently had the privilege of visiting Biophore India Pharmaceuticals and Vivere Imaging, exploring synergies that can advance safer MRI contrast solutions.
The visits focused on manufacturing partnerships, quality systems, and imaging workflows that could support the path from lab-scale innovation to clinical deployment.
Cross-industry collaboration is essential as we work toward scalable production and adoption of gadolinium-free contrast agents.

posts.title: Knowledgepie Pvt Ltd selected for NEC-IGNITE Cohort 3.0 by BioNEST-IITG
posts.excerpt: We are delighted to announce that Knowledgepie Private Limited has been selected for NEC-IGNITE Cohort 3.0 by BioNEST-IITG, accelerating our innovation journey.
longDescription: We are delighted to announce that Knowledgepie Private Limited has been selected for NEC-IGNITE Cohort 3.0 by BioNEST-IITG, accelerating our innovation journey.
The program provides mentorship, investor access, and translational support to help deep-tech startups move from proof-of-concept to market-ready products.
BioNEST-IITG continues to be a vital partner in nurturing biotechnology ventures from Assam and the wider Northeast region.

posts.title: Celebrating the Recognition of Our Director, Dr. Madhulekha Gogoi, by CSIR-NEIST
posts.excerpt: We are proud to share that our Director and Co-founder, Dr. Madhulekha Gogoi, has been recognized by CSIR-NEIST for her outstanding contribution to R&D.
longDescription: We are proud to share that our Director and Co-founder, Dr. Madhulekha Gogoi, has been recognized by CSIR-NEIST for her outstanding contribution to R&D.
Her work continues to anchor Knowledgepie's scientific foundation in nanomaterials, green chemistry, and the development of next-generation diagnostic agents.
This honour reflects years of dedicated research toward safer, more sustainable medical imaging technologies made in India.

posts.title: Knowledgepie at Advantage Assam 2.0: Showcasing Innovation from Assam to the World
posts.excerpt: Knowledgepie Private Limited took part in Advantage Assam 2.0 in Guwahati, showcasing green MRI contrast innovation from Assam to a global audience.
longDescription: Knowledgepie Private Limited took part in Advantage Assam 2.0 in Guwahati, showcasing green MRI contrast innovation from Assam to a global audience.
The summit connected policymakers, investors, and industry leaders—helping position Northeast India as a hub for biotechnology and advanced healthcare manufacturing.
Our booth highlighted XEUJ®, the green manufacturing route, and the clinical need for safer alternatives to conventional gadolinium-based agents.

posts.title: Knowledgepie at ISGAR: Sharing Advances in Safer MRI Contrast
posts.excerpt: Our team presented work on iron-based nanoparticle MRI contrast at ISGAR, engaging clinicians and researchers on safer alternatives to conventional GBCAs.
longDescription: Our team presented work on iron-based nanoparticle MRI contrast at ISGAR, engaging clinicians and researchers on safer alternatives to conventional GBCAs.
Discussions emphasized renal safety, sustainability, and the clinical need for contrast agents that balance performance with long-term patient wellbeing.
Conference feedback will inform our preclinical and regulatory roadmap as we prepare for the next stages of development.

posts.title: XEUJ®: Progress on a Green Route to Iron-Based MRI Contrast
posts.excerpt: An update on XEUJ® development—our iron-based nanoparticle contrast agent engineered via a sustainable manufacturing route for clinical reliability.
longDescription: An update on XEUJ® development—our iron-based nanoparticle contrast agent engineered via a sustainable manufacturing route for clinical reliability.
XEUJ® is designed as a gadolinium-free alternative with strong imaging performance, reduced environmental burden, and a manufacturing process aligned with green chemistry principles.
Ongoing work focuses on characterization, stability studies, and partnerships that will support eventual clinical evaluation.
